source.title: Advances in the neurobiological bases for food 'liking' versus 'wanting'

confidence_rationale: Comprehensive 2014 review consolidating a decade of hedonic-hotspot mapping across NAc shell and ventral pallidum, with anatomical specificity (rostrodorsal NAc shell hotspot ~1mm cubed; posterior VP hotspot in caudal half) and corresponding 'coldspots' where mu-opioid stimulation suppresses liking. Explicit distinction between hedonic generators (limited, spatially circumscribed) versus wanting/incentive generators (larger, more distributed). For MECH-295 this provides the substrate-level evidence that liking-stream is a real, distinct, anatomically discrete generator -- not a downstream readout of wanting. Confidence 0.74: review article (not primary), but consolidates the strongest available data with clear anatomical mapping. The necessity link is asserted theoretically but not directly tested in this paper.
failure_signatures: If liking and wanting were the same signal at different sites, no anatomical hotspot/coldspot dissociation should be observable; the review documents that they are.

If the liking-stream were a passive consequence of wanting activation, the same circuits should generate both; the review documents distinct generator regions and discrete control by mu-opioid, kappa-opioid, delta-opioid, cannabinoid and orexin signalling.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Across a decade of mapping, two anatomically discrete hedonic hotspots have been identified: the rostrodorsal NAc shell (~1 mm cubed) and a posterior VP region. Mu-opioid stimulation in either hotspot amplifies orofacial liking reactions; stimulation outside hotspots increases food intake without amplifying liking. Liking-generators are spatially limited and discrete; wanting/incentive generators are larger and overlap with but are not coextensive with the hotspots. Multiple receptor systems (mu-opioid, anandamide, orexin) gate the hotspots.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-295 requires that liking-stream activation be a separable, identifiable signal -- distinct from drive amplification (SD-012, hunger raising drive_level) and from wanting/incentive salience. Castro & Berridge 2014 supply the consolidated anatomical case that this is so. The hedonic hotspots are the substrate of MECH-295's z_liking node. Their dissociation from the larger wanting-supporting territory is the substrate-level analogue of MECH-295's architectural commitment that drive does not produce approach without liking activation.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This is a review, not primary data; its evidential weight is consolidated rather than independent. The review focuses on consummatory liking (orofacial reactions); the link to PRE-contact approach action selection (which is what MECH-295's approach_commit measures) is theoretically asserted but not the focus. The strongest test of MECH-295's necessity claim -- mu-opioid antagonist in the hotspot blocking drive-amplified approach to a learned cue -- is referenced as future work in this kind of literature but not delivered.
mapping.source_context: Review article surveying ~15 years of NAc shell and VP hotspot mapping, primarily rat data, with anatomical, pharmacological, and behavioural taste-reactivity evidence.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality moderate-high: Phys & Behav review by canonical authors, consolidating high-quality primary data. Mapping fidelity moderate-high: the paper documents the substrate MECH-295 needs but is not focused on the necessity link. Transfer risk moderate -- consummatory liking to pre-contact approach selection requires the incentive-learning bridge.
